summary: QG.1 is ready for implementation as a technical quality story. It is sourced to prior QA gate debt, has measurable acceptance criteria, and gives enough implementation and validation context to stabilize repository-wide gates before Story 1.3 PR/release enforcement relies on them.

template_compliance.notes: Required story sections are present, including Status, Executor Assignment, Story, Acceptance Criteria, CodeRabbit Integration, Tasks / Subtasks, Dev Notes, Testing, Change Log, Dev Agent Record, and QA Results. No template placeholders were found.

executor_assignment.notes: Executor @devops and quality gate @architect are distinct known agents with non-empty quality gate tools. The assignment matches the deployment/devtools nature of the story.

acceptance_criteria.notes: The six acceptance criteria are measurable against package scripts, test database behavior, pricing render behavior, focused regression checks, and CodeRabbit output.

source_verification.notes: Claims were checked against Story 1.1 and Story 1.2 QA gates, Story 1.3 testing notes, package.json scripts, resources/views/components/plans/one.blade.php, and database/migrations/2024_10_23_194329_usage_based_billing.php.

technical_guidance.notes: Tasks identify the PHPStan execution path, npm test database credential path, nullable meter pricing risk, scope boundaries, and regression commands.

testing_guidance.notes: Required verification commands cover CodeRabbit, repo-wide lint/typecheck/test gates, pricing smoke coverage, and focused memory story regressions.

security_scope.notes: The story does not introduce new auth, tenancy, secret, or payment-provider behavior beyond the narrow quality-gate and invalid pricing-render scenario.

coderabbit_integration.notes: core-config.yaml has CodeRabbit enabled, and the story includes readiness, uncommitted review, base-main review, blocking behavior for CRITICAL findings, and focus areas.

code_intelligence.notes: isCodeIntelAvailable returned unavailable in this session, so duplicate detection was skipped according to the validate-next-story fallback rule.

laravel_docs_check.notes: Laravel Boost documentation confirmed Sail-first command execution, Sail test equivalence to artisan test, and Sail npm command usage for the installed Laravel/Sail package context.

dev_readiness.notes: A development agent can implement the story without inventing requirements or expanding into Story 1.3 workspace settings, Story 1.4 diary record work, or broad billing-provider behavior.

non_blocking_notes: The local Story Development Cycle routes approved stories to @dev *develop, while QG.1's executor assignment records @devops because the domain is repository quality gates. Use @dev for the implementation command surface and preserve DevOps accountability for final pre-push/release gates.
Jira child story sync remains pending; the local story file is the source of truth until PO or DevOps explicitly syncs it.
Earlier memory stories still show CodeRabbit skip notices because they were drafted before CodeRabbit was enabled in core-config.yaml; QG.1 correctly uses the currently enabled CodeRabbit path.
